title: After-Action Report — UI re-theme, nav IA, mobile, custom-league baselines, web modularization
scope: The session opened as an open-ended "does this codebase need refactoring?" review, with three concrete asks from the user (later expanded to five): re-theme with light/dark toggle; grouped nav dropdowns; general refactoring and dead-code removal; mobile responsiveness of the universe-builder league table; baselines for the custom-league bias inputs.

rationale.item: The cream/navy/red "Baseball-Reference" theme read dated — move to a cohesive, WCAG-compliant, non-boilerplate palette with a light/dark toggle (the user specifically wanted a generated, readable scheme, not stock Bootstrap blue).
The top navigation was a flat list of 23 links with no grouping — hard to scan. Wanted grouped dropdowns.
Mobile responsiveness "still not optimal" — the universe-builder league table overflowed horizontally on a phone, cutting off the right-hand columns.
The custom-league bias inputs gave no sense of baselines: bare 0-default boxes with no indication of neutral, range, or what "cranking up" an attribute does to league output.

The theme was already centralized in CSS custom properties in o27v2/web/templates/base.html, so a re-theme was low-risk and high-impact; o27/almanac/static/almanac.css already had a [data-theme="dark"] pattern to reuse.
o27v2/web/app.py is an 8,336-line monolith (84 routes, 168 defs) — the one real "god file". lib/ (TypeScript Zod schemas + a React API client) was unused — an abandoned rewrite scaffold.

delegation_events.decision: Extract only the safe formatters slice this session rather than splitting all 84 routes into blueprints
delegation_events.quote: I deliberately did only the safe, fully-verifiable slice (pure formatters) this session — see Follow-ups for why.

actions.description: Removed dead lib/ scaffold: four unused workspace packages, tsconfig project references, pnpm-workspace globs; refreshed pnpm-lock.yaml

Re-themed UI into "Twilight Diamond" palette with split theme-flipping and fixed --fill-* tokens, [data-theme="dark"] override block, persisted light/dark toggle with pre-paint script, themeable flash banners
Grouped nav into 6 dropdowns (Games / Players / Stats / League / History / Manage) with active-state highlighting and inline mobile sections; endpoint names and url_for targets unchanged
Universe builder responsive: below 768px #league-table opts out of the min-width floor and stacks each league into a labelled card via data-label + media query

Custom-league builder baselines: labelled sliders with live ± readout, per-knob hints, 0 = neutral · −25…+25 legend, collapsible style reference, per-row "Start from preset" picker; metadata sourced from o27v2.league via _universe_custom_meta
Began breaking up web monolith: extracted nine pure presentation filters into o27v2/web/formatters.py, re-registered as Jinja filters from app.py

risk_posture: Endpoint names and `url_for` targets are unchanged.
No behavior change; `app` and all endpoint names stay put so the many cross-module importers (`manage.py`, `sim.py`, `season_archive.py`, tests) are untouched.

CSS-only mobile stacking for the universe table (vs. rewriting the JS-cloned <template> into card markup) — keeps the row-serialization JS and the server's positional form parsing completely untouched, which is the lower-risk path.
The Dockerfile is pure-Python, so this has zero deploy impact.

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_template_renders.py (renders base.html on every page) — green throughout
Ad-hoc test-client renders of /universe/new confirmed theme toggle, dark block, 6 nav dropdowns with active-state, mobile stacking CSS, style reference + sliders + presets
validation_claims.check_method: Render /universe/new via Flask test client and grep for the elements

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end POST to /universe/new with a custom-bias league: 302 redirect, custom bias JSON persisted to teams.style_profile
validation_claims.check_method: POST via test client and inspect teams.style_profile

validation_claims.claim: Full suites: 213 passed, 7 skipped. The only failures (10) are test_stat_invariants.py erroring with no such table: games — a pre-existing environment issue

non_actions.item: Route → blueprint split of web/app.py (the bulk of the refactor)
non_actions.reason: It touches all 84 routes, and this environment can't browser-verify the result; the automated tests cover only a handful of routes.

non_actions.item: Aligning almanac palette hues to the new "Twilight Diamond" values
non_actions.reason: almanac.css already has its own light/dark tokens; a later pass should align

non_actions.item: Broader mobile audit of the widest stat tables on a real device
non_actions.reason: the universe table was the screenshotted offender and is fixed

residual_risks: Verification had no browser and no populated DB — done via the Flask test client and test suites only.
open_hooks: The formatters.py extraction establishes the pattern (extract + re-export, no endpoint churn); aggregations.py (the _aggregate_* / _pitcher_wl_map / _PSTATS_DEDUP_SQL cluster) is the next clean extraction target.
Blueprint-by-blueprint incremental split on a branch with staging / browser verification.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — UI re-theme, nav IA, mobile, custom-league baselines, web modularization
unmapped_content: Decisions & tradeoffs details: fixed-fill color tokens are the cleanest way to get a dark mode that passes contrast on solid controls without per-component overrides; sliders over number boxes communicate "0 is neutral, here's the range" better than a 0-default text field. Mobile root cause: a global .table-responsive .dense-table { min-width: 720px } floor plus an inline min-width:640px. Predecessor: aar-custom-league-editing.md. Branch claude/codebase-refactor-design-IgnTD. Each workstream is a separate commit on the branch.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed: 2026-05-25'. Clean AAR shape.
